subClaimText: Del Monte Pacific Limited deconsolidated its U.S. Del Monte Foods business effective May 2025 and classified it as discontinued operations.
sourceTitle: Del Monte Pacific Limited FY2025 annual report and FY2025 results materials describing classification of the U.S. Del Monte Foods business as discontinued operations and deconsolidation effective May 2025.
urlOrCitation: Del Monte Pacific FY2025 annual report and SGX/PSE disclosure filings (https://www.delmontepacific.com/investors)
notes: Anchor cited in claims-source-pack.json under C-006 and restated in plan Quick Source Anchors. Underlying filing not yet retrieved into repo, so verdict is supported_by_anchor pending Gate 1.

subClaimText: The deconsolidated U.S. Del Monte Foods entity filed for Chapter 11 in U.S. Bankruptcy Court.
sourceTitle: Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing and supporting docket records.
publisher: U.S. Bankruptcy Court (PACER docket) / Del Monte Foods press release
urlOrCitation: PACER bankruptcy docket for Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 case and corresponding press release (PACER + Del Monte Foods newsroom)
notes: Anchor cited in claims-source-pack.json under C-006 and restated in plan Quick Source Anchors. Q-004 / Q-DMC-07 still asks which exact entity (Del Monte Foods Inc., Del Monte Foods Holdings, or an intermediate) filed Chapter 11.

subClaimText: Del Monte retreated from destructive private-label vegetable canning competition.
sourceTitle: Del Monte Pacific Limited FY2025 annual report and FY2025 results materials.
urlOrCitation: Del Monte Pacific FY2025 annual report and SGX/PSE disclosure filings (https://www.delmontepacific.com/investors)
notes: Deconsolidation is directionally consistent with private-label retreat but is not a direct quote. Indirect-only evidence is insufficient under acceptance rule.

subClaimText: Del Monte retreated from destructive private-label vegetable canning competition.
sourceTitle: Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing.
publisher: U.S. Bankruptcy Court (PACER docket) / Del Monte Foods press release
urlOrCitation: PACER bankruptcy docket for Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 case and corresponding press release
notes: Chapter 11 may preserve capacity under new ownership; absent retrieved post-petition disclosures the inference is indirect.

subClaimText: Del Monte Foods (U.S.) underwent a 2014 ownership change in which it was acquired from a U.S. parent by Del Monte Pacific Limited.
notes: No primary source for the 2014 transaction is in the existing pack. Open question Q-DMC-01.

subClaimText: Following the 2014 ownership change Del Monte Foods pursued aggressive private-label and branded price competition in U.S. canned vegetables.
notes: No retailer testimony, antitrust filing, or trade-press interview is in the source register. Open question Q-DMC-02.

subClaimText: Del Monte's acquisition of Sager Creek Vegetable Company produced operating, integration, or write-down issues.
notes: No Del Monte Foods filing, Del Monte Pacific MD&A excerpt, or impairment disclosure for Sager Creek is in the source register. Open question Q-DMC-03.

subClaimText: Del Monte Foods executed multi-year restructuring programs (cost reduction, write-downs, refinancings) prior to the May 2025 deconsolidation.
notes: No pre-2025 restructuring announcement is sourced in the pack. May fold under S-003 once retrieved. Open question Q-DMC-04.

subClaimText: Del Monte closed or sold canned-vegetable and related plants as part of capacity rationalization.
notes: No plant-level source (county records, USDA inspection-list changes, local press, or filing-level disclosure) is in the source register. Open question Q-DMC-05.

subClaimText: Contrary: a Chapter 11 reorganization frequently preserves operating capacity and customer contracts under new ownership.
sourceTitle: Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing.
publisher: U.S. Bankruptcy Court (PACER docket) / Del Monte Foods press release
urlOrCitation: PACER bankruptcy docket for Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 case
notes: Without retrieved post-petition operating-plan disclosures, asset-purchase agreements, or stalking-horse bids, the pack does not adjudicate whether U.S. Del Monte canned-vegetable capacity is being shut down or transferred.

subClaimText: Contrary: improved market structure also requires that other private-label competitors (notably Lakeside Foods) do not absorb prior Del Monte volume on aggressive terms.
sourceTitle: Lakeside Foods public materials referencing the ~90% private-label vegetable canning quote.
publisher: Lakeside Foods, Inc. (corporate communications / interview transcript)
urlOrCitation: Lakeside Foods website and quoted industry interview material
notes: Cross-competitor dynamic is out of scope for this test but is a contrary risk to the conduct-change thesis. Tracked under C-005 / Q-001 in the source pack.

subClaimText: Contrary: even if confirmed, the 2014 ownership change does not by itself imply price aggression; Del Monte Pacific strategy could be framed as branded-share defense.
notes: Without S-003 retrieved, this test cannot adjudicate which framing applies.

subClaimText: Contrary: plan Gate 1 requires a retrieved source artifact before any claim is admitted to the final memo. Most sub-claims here rely on anchors only or no source.
sourceTitle: SENEA Diligence plan, Decision Gates section.
notes: Plan-level admissibility argument: the family should not yet be admitted to the verdict memo until retrieval is complete.
